medications.indication_rationale: Symptomatic relief of grittiness; source advised preservative-free lubricants every ~2 hours.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1 drop to each eye every ~2 hours as needed (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Soothe the eyes; source advised 4 times daily.
Antibiotics don't treat viral conjunctivitis; reserve for clearly bacterial disease.
Topical steroids can worsen undiagnosed herpes keratitis and need specialist supervision — not for self-directed use.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Strict contagion control: wash hands frequently, use single-use tissues (discard immediately), don't share towels/pillows (hot-wash them), avoid touching/rubbing the eyes, and no contact lenses until the eyes are white for 48h.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Viral conjunctivitis is highly contagious for up to ~2 weeks; hygiene prevents spread and reinfection.

patient_education_points.point: This is viral conjunctivitis ('pink eye') — basically a cold in the eyes from the same virus as your sore throat. Antibiotics won't help; it settles over 1–3 weeks. Lubricant drops and cool compresses ease the grittiness.

It's very contagious — wash your hands, use single-use tissues and bin them straight away, don't share towels/pillows, avoid rubbing your eyes, and don't wear contact lenses until your eyes are white for 48 hours.

Go to urgent eye care / ED if your vision is actually reduced (not just blurred by tears), you get severe deep eye pain, strong light sensitivity or halos around lights — especially if you wear contact lenses.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution; reconsider bacterial if discharge becomes thick purulent; red-eye danger signs.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Reduced vision, severe pain, photophobia or halos — urgent eye care.

must_include_items: Recognise viral conjunctivitis; supportive care (lubricants, compresses), NO antibiotics
Red-eye danger-sign safety-net (reduced vision, severe pain, photophobia, halos; contact-lens risk)
Strict contagion control (hand hygiene, single-use tissues, no shared towels, no contact lenses)
acceptable_alternatives: Lubricants ± cool compresses; antihistamine drops if an allergic component
errors_of_commission: Routine antibiotic drops for viral conjunctivitis
Missing a sight-threatening red eye / contact-lens keratitis
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires supportive (non-antibiotic) management, a red-eye danger-sign safety-net, and strict contagion control.
